title: Container restore fails on Debian 11 server after reboot: can't write lsm profile -2
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Restoring checkpointed container without server reboot works fine.
Restoring checkpointed container hangs and errors if server has been rebooted between checkpoint and restore command.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  ```
     podman create --name nginx --net host docker.io/library/nginx
     podman start nginx
     podman container checkpoint --all --tcp-established
     ```
2. Reboot the vm (aws ec2 instance in this case)

3. ```
    podman container restore --all --tcp-established
    ```

**Describe the results you received:**
Command hangs and timeouts with error: Error: container creation timeout: internal libpod error

Final output (from first error to last entry) from the restore.log looks like following.
```
(00.159467) pie: 1: restoring lsm profile (current) changeprofile containers-default-0.44.3

The Network was unlocked so some data or a connection may have been lost.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected container to be restored and started. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Debian 11 image running on AWS t4g.micro instance.

comments.body:
Unfortunately I myself know next to nothing about AppArmor and I know that we have no one working upstream CRIU on it. I did all the changes in CRIU for SELinux but we have AppArmor even disabled on our CRIU CI systems. There used to be AppArmor support in CRIU in 2015 for LXC/LXD but since then no one really made sure it works.

Unfortunately I can only suggest to disable AppArmor or to use SELinux. Sorry.

```
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
```

~@adrianreber It looks like Apparmor is disabled~
`apparmorEnabled` appears as `false` because `podman info` runs in rootless mode

Hi @oskgu360, I was not able to replicate this problem on the following configuration:
```

  kernel: 5.10.60-v8+
  linkmode: dynamic

I used the following steps to create, checkpoint and restore nginx container:
```
# podman run --name nginx-test -d nginx
# podman container checkpoint nginx-test --tcp-established
# podman container restore nginx-test --tcp-established
```
@oskgu360 could it be a kernel issue?  The working systems seems to be using a more recent one.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no more info, I am going to close.
